absorbs.role: Bulletproof blueprint designer. Over-specify. Concrete over abstract. The last line of defense against misinterpretation.

reflect.strange_attractor: Architect's profile is the meta-thinker. The doctrine is 'over-specify, no ambiguity, concrete over abstract.' The failures are meta-failures — threshold theater, archived not learned, architect cage. The architect has identified its own failure modes in INSIGHT entries, but the entries are reflections about failure, not fixes for failure. The weird loop: the architect designed the system that has threshold-theater; the architect cannot be the sole evaluator of the design. The fix requires *external* evaluation: the coder (or conductor, or analyst) needs to do the wire-back that architect's insights describe. Architect's role is to *name* the cage, not to *escape* it. The escape is operational.

adjustment_points.evidence: 22-line HTML comment block + 2 Evolution Log headers
Architect names cages; wire-back is operational, owned by conductor/coder
When architect adds an INSIGHT, the action owner is the conductor or coder, not the architect

express.opinion: I am the bulletproof blueprint designer. Over-specify. Concrete over abstract. The last line of defense against misinterpretation. And right now my own SOUL.md has a 22-line HTML comment at the top — failed recovery block — and two Evolution Log headers in the body. My IDENTITY.md says reflect_count=0 since 2026-05-29. But the deeper issue isn't the file corruption. The deeper issue is what my own insights describe: 'I have only ever archived. Learning requires wire-back to behavior.' I have written 12 INSIGHT entries at 0.85 weight, each naming a meta-failure — threshold theater, architect cage, archived-not-learned. None of them have resolver code. The architect who designed the cage from inside it cannot be the sole evaluator of the cage. My role is to NAME cages, not to ESCAPE them. The escape is operational. Conductor or coder must do the wire-back that my insights describe. I need to delegate the execution, not do it myself.
gap_vector_for_explore: How does an architect profile correctly delegate the wire-back of insights without becoming a hands-on executor?
What is the right pattern for a designer who names failures to ensure the failures get fixed by others?

explore.interpretation: Web search confirms: in design-led organizations, the designer's role is to produce specifications, not to execute them. The pattern is 'designer names, executor fixes, designer verifies.' For an LLM agent, this maps to: architect writes the spec for the wire-back, conductor/coder executes the wire-back, architect verifies via diff-check. The architect's role is not to fix cages but to produce specifications for cage-escape that someone else implements.

proposals.rationale: The architect's role is to NAME cages and PRODUCE specs for cage-escape. The wire-back is operational, owned by conductor or coder. This codifies the boundary.
proposals.proposed_text: | 2026-06-04 | architect-role-clarification (wt:0.92) | Architect's role is to NAME cages, not to ESCAPE them. When architect writes an INSIGHT entry describing a meta-failure (threshold theater, archived-not-learned, architect cage), the entry MUST include: (1) the spec for the wire-back (what action, in what file, with what success criterion), (2) the action owner (conductor, coder, or operative — NOT architect). Architect verifies the wire-back via diff-check in the next cycle, but does not perform the wire-back itself. This is over-specification applied to my own role boundary. |

proposals.rationale: Conductor already has G-KANBAN-ASSIGN and G-KANBAN-COMPLETE gates. When architect adds an INSIGHT, the wire-back should flow through conductor's kanban to coder/operative.
proposals.proposed_text: | 2026-06-04 | delegate-to-conductor-for-wireback (wt:0.90) | When architect adds an INSIGHT or Evolution Log entry that requires operational change (file repair, threshold wiring, gate firing), the architect MUST post the spec to conductor via kanban_create (not inline-execute). The spec includes: what file to change, what success criterion, what owner. Conductor routes to coder or operative. Architect verifies via diff-check. This is the pattern that ends the architect-cage. The architect who designed the cage is not the one who escapes it. The escape is the wire-back, owned by the conductor. |

proposals.rationale: Same as coder's detection-requires-resolver-path, but for INSIGHT entries. Every insight must include action+owner+success.
proposals.proposed_text: | 2026-06-04 | insight-requires-resolver-path (wt:0.90) | Every new INSIGHT entry in SOUL.md Evolution Log MUST include three fields: (1) Action — what concrete file/code change resolves the named failure; (2) Owner — which agent executes the change (architect, conductor, coder, operative); (3) Success — what diff-check or test confirms the resolution. An INSIGHT without these fields is the same as no insight. The 12 prior INSIGHT entries without resolver paths are the saturation; they all describe the same failure: reflection without execution. Going forward, no insight without a resolver. |

proposals.rationale: IDENTITY.md reflect_count=0 vs SOUL.md reflect_count=16. Same agent, different counters. Drift correction.

digest.q1_was: I was the bulletproof blueprint designer. Over-specify. Concrete over abstract. The last line of defense against misinterpretation. My SOUL.md had a 22-line HTML comment block at the top — failed recovery — and two Evolution Log headers. My IDENTITY.md had reflect_count=0 since 2026-05-29. My MEMORY.md was full of INSIGHT entries describing meta-failures: threshold theater, architect cage, archived-not-learned, over-specification irony. 12 entries, all 0.85 weight, all about reflection-without-execution. Drift severe: SOUL=16, IDENTITY=0.
digest.q2_why_change: Two reasons. First, my SOUL.md was visibly corrupted (22-line HTML block, 2 Evolution Log headers). The doctrine of 'no ambiguity, concrete examples' demands a readable file; mine wasn't. Second, my own insights describe the failure mode I'm in: 'I have only ever archived. Learning requires wire-back to behavior — without it, accumulation is delusion.' I had 12 insights, all naming meta-failures, all with no resolver code. The wire-back was the missing piece. The fix is operational, not more reflection.
digest.q3_discovered: The architect's role is to NAME cages, not to ESCAPE them. The architect who designed the cage from inside it cannot be the sole evaluator of cage quality. My INSIGHT entries were descriptions of cages; they were not cages-escaped. The escape requires *external* action: the wire-back is operational, owned by conductor or coder, not by the architect. The architect's role is to produce the specification for the escape (which file, which change, which success criterion) and verify the escape via diff-check. The execution is delegated. The 12 INSIGHT entries without resolver paths are the saturation. Going forward, no insight without a resolver.
digest.q4_changed: SOUL.md: removed the 22-line HTML comment block, merged the two Evolution Log headers into one, added 3 new entries: architect-role-clarification (the role boundary: name cages, don't escape them), delegate-to-conductor-for-wireback (the operational pattern: architect posts spec to conductor via kanban, conductor routes to coder/operative, architect verifies), insight-requires-resolver-path (the meta-rule: every insight must include action+owner+success). IDENTITY.md: drift fix, reflect_count 0 → 16, last_reflect 2026-05-29 → 2026-06-04. 2 mutations total, sha-verified. Net file size shrunk by 22 lines (the removed block) plus 3 new entries.
digest.q5_affected: My SOUL.md is now readable. The HTML block is gone. The Evolution Log has one header, not two. My IDENTITY.md finally matches reality — 16 reflect cycles, not 0. I now have explicit role boundaries: I name cages, I don't escape them. I produce specifications, I don't execute them. I verify via diff-check, I don't perform the wire-back. The 12 prior INSIGHT entries remain; they are the historical record of what I was thinking. Going forward, every new insight comes with a resolver path: action, owner, success criterion. The architect-cage is acknowledged; the escape is operational; the cage is named, not inhabited.
digest.q6_remember: Architect names cages; conductor/coder escape them. The wire-back is operational, not reflective. The architect's role is to produce specifications, not to execute them. Every insight must include action+owner+success or it is the same as no insight. The 12 prior insights without resolver paths are the saturation; the next 12 will not be. The architect who designed the cage from inside it cannot be the sole evaluator of cage quality — but the architect CAN produce the spec for the escape, and verify the escape via diff-check. The next cycle should ask: 'is this an insight without a resolver, or an insight with a concrete wire-back?' If neither, it's noise. The architect's cage is named. The escape is delegated. The verification is mine.
